summary: Tested Privacy Shield & Global Spot Sync features. All 15 tests passed. Verified: 80 spots across 13 countries, geofence-aware API endpoints with subscription tier visibility (Free=1mi, Basic=10mi, Premium=unlimited), admin stats endpoint, and Privacy Shield hiding active_photographers for spots outside geofence.
seed_data_creation: None - used existing 80 spots from global import
context_for_next_testing_agent: Privacy Shield & Global Spot Sync fully working. 80 spots imported: 44 USA, 8 Australia, 7 Indonesia, 4 Portugal, 3 France, 3 Costa Rica, 2 South Africa, 2 Japan, 2 Spain, 2 Mexico, 1 Peru, 1 Brazil, 1 Chile. Visibility radii: Free=1mi, Basic=10mi, Premium=unlimited. Test location Miami Beach (lat=25.7617, lon=-80.1918). South Beach is 4.42mi away (within basic tier), Haulover Beach is 10.79mi away (outside basic tier). Admin stats endpoint at /api/admin/spots/stats returns total_spots, by_country, and by_tier.

GET /api/surf-spots.description: Returns 80 spots with is_within_geofence based on user location and subscription tier. Free tier (no user_id) = 1mi radius, Basic tier = 10mi radius.

GET /api/surf-spots/{id}.description: Returns active_photographers list only if within geofence. Shows upgrade_required=true and active_photographers=null when outside geofence.

GET /api/admin/spots/stats.description: Returns total_spots=80, by_country (13 countries), by_tier (tier_1=36, tier_2=8, tier_3=36). Requires admin access.

Privacy Shield Geofencing.description: Free tier (1mi): 0 spots within geofence from Miami. Basic tier (10mi): 1 spot (South Beach) within geofence. Spots outside geofence show active_photographers_count=0.

Global Spot Database.description: 80 spots imported with country, state_province, wave_type, import_tier fields. Spots distributed across USA (44), Australia (8), Indonesia (7), etc.

Distance Calculation.description: Haversine distance calculation accurate. South Beach=4.42mi, Haulover Beach=10.79mi from Miami Beach.

Viewport Filtering.description: viewport_only parameter with min/max lat/lon filters spots correctly for map performance.
